data.title: As a practitioner, epic validation routes failures to the correct planning tier based on gap type
data.description:
## Description

**What**: At epic validation before closure, failing success criteria are classified as intent gaps (route to brainstorm with user confirmation) or implementation gaps (route to implementation-plan in epic-direct mode).
**Why**: Today, epic validation failure is a dead end — the practitioner must manually diagnose whether the problem is ambiguous intent or missing implementation. Automated routing enables the self-healing loop to close the gap without manual intervention (for implementation gaps) or with targeted user input (for intent gaps).
**Scope**:
- IN: Gap classification logic (compare criterion text against actual codebase behavior), routing to brainstorm (intent gap, user confirmation required) vs implementation-plan epic-direct mode (implementation gap, autonomous), integration with the re-invocation guard (Story 1) and REPLAN_ESCALATE signal (Story 2)
- OUT: Sprint orchestrator integration (separate epic 9d3e-957d), the actual brainstorm or implementation-plan execution

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, epic validation can classify a failing success criterion as either an intent gap (criterion text does not match what implementation does) or an implementation gap (criterion is clear but not fully satisfied)
  ← Satisfies: "if a failing success criterion's text does not match what the implementation actually does (intent gap), route to brainstorm"
- When this story is complete, intent gaps produce a brainstorm routing recommendation with user confirmation required, and implementation gaps produce an implementation-plan routing recommendation in epic-direct mode
  ← Satisfies: "route to brainstorm with user confirmation... route to implementation-plan in epic-direct mode to create new tasks"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Testing] Gap classification is an LLM judgment call — needs eval/test scenarios with clear intent gap vs implementation gap examples
- [Maintainability] Classification heuristic must be documented so future maintainers understand the routing decision

## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
